title: Clipboard Supervisor
description: A walkthrough of the Clipboard source and the supervisor model for newly copied images.

steps.title: Open Vision -> Clipboard
steps.body: Clipboard is a deliberate image source. If its tab is missing, include Clipboard in the Vision source selector on the Core page first.

steps.title: Clipboard Source
steps.body: The Source tab controls how copied images enter NC: attach to the next turn, send immediately, or feed the hidden Vision loop. For supervisor-style behavior, hidden Vision loop is usually the interesting route.

steps.title: Deliberate vs Ambient
steps.body: Clipboard differs from Screen because copied images are deliberate user choices. That makes clipboard-triggered reactions feel more confident and less like ambient background commentary.

steps.title: Supervisor Tab
steps.body: Switch to the Supervisor child tab. This is where you define persona-driven reactions to newly copied images, broad clipboard-image patterns, or a very specific kind of reference image.

steps.title: NEW Clipboard Images
steps.body: Clipboard Supervisor distinguishes between a genuinely NEW clipboard image and one that is unchanged from the last cycle. That helps it comment once without nagging forever.

steps.title: Recommended Use
steps.body: This addon is a strong fit for copied inspiration images, screenshots, artwork, articles, memes, and anything the user intentionally places on the clipboard for NC to notice.
